data.title: Feiyang International Shareholders to Vote March 20 on Acquisition Termination
data.description:
Shareholders of Feiyang International Holdings Group will meet March 20 to vote on the proposed termination of a purchase contract by unit Zhejiang Feiyang International Travel Group, a Feb. 21 bourse filing said.

The unit had previously agreed to acquire a 60% equity stake in Zhejiang Feijiada Aviation Service from Ningbo Zhenhang Business Service.

Under the termination agreement, Zhejiang Feiyang International Travel Group will sell the 60% stake back to Ningbo Zhenhang Business as certain performance conditions for the acquisition failed to be fulfilled.

Shares of the company were down nearly 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Just Eat Takeaway posts growth in 2024 core profit
data.description:
Europe's biggest meal delivery firm Just Eat Takeaway.com on Monday reported a rise in its full-year core profit driven by an improvement in the key UK and Ireland markets, mainly due to higher cost per order and marketing.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization (EBITDA) rose to 460 million euros ($484 million) in 2024 from 339 million a year earlier, Just Eat said.

($1 = 0.9512 euros)

data.title: China Satellite Communications Launches New Satellite
data.description:
China Satellite Communications on Feb. 22 launched the ChinaSat 10R satellite from the Xichang Satellite Launch Center using a Long March 3B enhanced rocket.

The satellite has entered its designated orbit and will undergo in-orbit testing before being officially handed over for operations, according to a Monday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

ChinaSat 10R replaces the ChinaSat 10 satellite while significantly enhancing capacity, the company said.

Shares of the aerospace company rose 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Sun Silver Starts Environmental Surveys at Nevada Silver-Gold Project
data.description:
Sun Silver began extensive baseline environmental surveys at its Maverick Springs silver-gold project in Nevada to collect data for environmental assessments and regulatory compliance under the National Environmental Policy Act, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The surveys will run from February to August. The final report is expected to be received by November, the filing said.

The surveys will include vegetation surveys, wildlife surveys, species-specific habitat assessments, and wetland and habitat confirmation, per the filing.

The firm is also focused on progressing mineral resource expansion programs and feasibility studies.

The firm's shares fell 4% on market close on Monday.

data.title: Empower Wins District Cooling Contract for UAE Smart District Expansion
data.description:
Emirates Central Cooling Systems (DFM:EMPOWER), d/b/a Empower, signed a deal with Dubai Multi Commodities Centre for district cooling services at Uptown Dubai's next phase.

The United Arab Emirates-based cooling services company said Monday that it will provide 24,675 refrigeration tons of cooling capacity for the second phase of the mixed-use development.

Empower supplied district cooling for the project's first phase, delivering 3,850 refrigeration tons of cooling capacity.

data.title: Prosus to Buy Just Eat Takeaway for $4.29 Billion to Create EU Food Delivery Champion
data.description:
(06:00 GMT) *Prosus to Buy Just Eat Takeaway.com
(06:00 GMT) *Prosus to Buy Just Eat Takeaway.com for EUR4.1B
(06:01 GMT) *Prosus to Buy Just Eat Takeaway.com
(06:02 GMT) *Prosus, Just Eat Takeaway.com Agree on Recommended All-Cash Offer
(06:03 GMT) *Prosus Offers Just Eat Takeaway.com Holders EUR20.30 a Share in Cash
(06:04 GMT) *Prosus: Offer Supported, Recommended by Just Eat Takeaway's Management, Supervisory Boards
(06:04 GMT) *Prosus Says It Sees Opportunity to Accelerate Growth at Just Eat Takeaway.com
(06:05 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com CEO Says Prosus Supports Company's Strategic Plans
(06:06 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com CEO Says Prosus Resources Will Help to Accelerate Investments, Growth
(06:07 GMT) *Prosus: Acquisition Complements Existing Food-Delivery Footprint Outside of Europe
(06:24 GMT) Prosus to Buy Just Eat Takeaway for $4.29 Billion to Create EU Food Delivery Champion

By Najat Kantouar

Amsterdam-listed investment group Prosus agreed to buy Just Eat Takeaway.com for 4.1 billion euros ($4.29 billion) in cash to create the fourth largest food delivery group globally.

Prosus said it reached a conditional agreement to acquire the food-delivery group for 20.30 euros a share in cash. This represents a 49% premium to the shares' three-month volume weighted average price and a 22% premium to its three-month high.

Prosus said it sees an opportunity to accelerate growth at Just Eat through the deal, supported by its strong industry experience to innovate and drive efficiencies.

"We are excited for Just Eat Takeaway.com to join the Prosus Group and the opportunity to create a European tech champion. Prosus already has an extensive food delivery portfolio outside of Europe and a proven track record of profitable growth through investment in our customer and driver experiences, restaurant partnerships, and world-class logistics, powered by innovation and AI," Prosus Chief Executive Officer Fabricio Bloisi said.

The offer was unanimously supported by Just Eat's management board and supervisory Board. The deal is subject to regulatory approvals.

"Just Eat Takeaway.com is now a faster growing, more profitable and predominantly European-based business. Prosus fully supports our strategic plans, and its extensive resources will help to further accelerate our investments and growth across food, groceries, fintech and other adjacencies," Just Eat Chief executive Officer Jitse Groen said.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Saudi's Amak Declares Higher H2 2024 Dividend
data.description:
Al Masane Al Kobra Mining (SASE:1322), d/b/a Amak, on Monday, declared a cash dividend of 1.18 Saudi riyals for the second half of 2024.

The Saudi Arabia-listed mining company will pay the dividend to shareholders on record on March 10, with the distribution date to be disclosed later. It paid 0.65 riyal per share a year ago.

data.title: Amgen plans $200 million investment in India site, CEO says
data.description: U.S. drugmaker Amgen will invest about $200 million this year in its new technology centre in southern India, with further investments planned, Chief Executive Officer Robert A. Bradway said at the inauguration of the site.

data.title: Silver Prices Rise as Dollar Weakens
data.description:
Silver prices rose above $32.50 per ounce on Monday, recovering some of the losses from the previous session as the dollar came under pressure due to a deteriorating economic outlook in the US and a stronger euro.

Ongoing trade and geopolitical uncertainties continued to fuel safe-haven demand for the precious metal.

Last week, US President Donald Trump’s threat to impose tariffs on key sectors, including cars, semiconductors, pharmaceuticals, and lumber, keeping markets on edge.

On Friday, Trump also confirmed that Russia had indeed invaded Ukraine, while announcing that Kyiv would soon sign a minerals agreement with the US as part of efforts to resolve the conflict.

Meanwhile, in Germany, the conservative Christian Democratic Union won the general election, with leader Friedrich Merz expected to form a coalition government focused on fiscal reform.

data.title: Diös to Lease Office Space in Sweden to Credit Provider Almi
data.description:
Diös Fastigheter agreed to lease more than 1,500 square meters of office space in Östersund, Sweden, to state-owned credit company Almi.

Almi will share the property at Hamngatan with the shopping center Kärnan, according to a Monday release. The Swedish real estate company said the new space will be used for the consolidation of Almi's regional operations in the country into a single office, with occupancy expected in September.

data.title: New York Times business news - Feb 24
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the New York Times business pages. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- Asteroid mining company AstroForge is aiming to heave a spacecraft toward an asteroid later this week, in a goal to kick off a future where precious metals are mined around the solar system to create vast fortunes on Earth.

- Joy Reid's evening news show on MSNBC is being canceled, part of a far-reaching programming overhaul orchestrated by Rebecca Kutler, the network’s new president.

- Trump administration appointees in charge of the U.S. Agency for International Development sent employees an email saying that they were laying off 2,000 workers and putting up to thousands of foreign service officers and other direct hires around the world on paid leave.

- Several Trump-appointed agency leaders urged federal workers not to comply with Elon Musk's order to summarize their accomplishments for the past week or be removed from their positions, even as Musk doubled down on his demand over the weekend.

data.title: Indian rupee premiums, short-term bond yields drop after central bank's $10 bln FX swap
data.description:
The 1-year dollar/rupee forward premium plunged on Monday and shorter-term India government bond yields fell, after the country's central bank announced an FX swap to shore up rupee liquidity.

WHY IT'S IMPORTANT

Forward premiums are a factor that companies take into account while making their decision on hedging their foreign exchange risks.

A drop in forward premiums reduces the hedging cost of dollar payments that are due in the future.

Meanwhile, lower shorter-duration yields are beneficial for corporates and non-banking financial companies as they mainly borrow through such papers. Corporate bond yields track government counterparts, and a drop in yields reduces the borrowing cost of companies.

CONTEXT

The Reserve Bank of India will conduct a $10 billion 3-year buy/sell swap on Friday, which would infuse around 870 billion rupees of liquidity in the banking system.

The move comes at a time when the banking sector has been reeling a under large cash deficit, which is expected to persist.

The RBI has already infused over 3.6 trillion rupees through a combination of open market and secondary market bond purchases, a six-month FX swap and repos that mature in early April.

MARKET REACTION

The 1-year dollar/rupee forward premium dropped 12 basis points to 1.99%, its lowest since the first week of December.

The benchmark 10-year bond yield (IN10YT=RR) was barely changed, while yields on the four-year to five-year bonds were down 2 basis points, with traders anticipating further declines.

The 1-year overnight index swap (OIS) rate fell to near a 30-month low.

KEY QUOTES

"Assuming FX intervention, tax outflows and currency leakage in the last month of the financial year, the RBI will have to do open market purchases of around 1 trillion rupees to keep durable liquidity near neutral levels going into the new financial year," said Alok Singh, group head of treasury at CSB Bank.

"Durable liquidity could remain in a deficit of 300-500 billion rupees range. This would suggest need for further liquidity support from RBI in March to the tune of 500 billion–1 trillion rupees on our estimates," Citi's chief India economist Samiran Chakraborty said.

CHART

($1 = 86.6770 Indian rupees)

data.title: Market Chatter: Japan's Digital Deficit Triples in a Decade as US Tech Dominates
data.description:
Japan's digital trade deficit surged to a record 6.46 trillion yen in 2024, more than tripling from 2.02 trillion yen in 2014, as reliance on US tech giants grew, Kyodo News reported Monday.

The shortfall, driven by payments for cloud services, online ads, and licensing, reflects Japanese firms' dependence on Amazon, Microsoft, and Google to boost efficiency amid a lack of domestic alternatives.

Efforts to develop local cloud services continue, but Japan lags in digital capabilities, said Mitsubishi Research Institute's Kengo Wataya. The deficit is unlikely to narrow soon, he said, adding that Japan must find ways to generate digital revenue elsewhere.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Singapore Inflation Eases in January
data.description:
Singapore's MAS core inflation, which excludes the cost of accommodation and private transport, fell to 0.8% year on year in January, from 1.7% in December 2024, due to lower inflation across all broad core categories.

Headline consumer price index, or overall inflation, eased to 1.2% on year, from 1.5% in December 2024, due to a moderation in accommodation inflation, according to a joint release by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) and the Ministry of Trade and Industry on Monday.

On a month on month basis, core inflation declined by 0.2%, while headline inflation declined by 0.7%, the data showed.

MAS Core Inflation is projected to average 1.0% to 2.0% during 2025, while overall inflation is expected to average 1.5% to 2.5% for the year with private transport inflation expected to pickup.

data.title: Vection Technologies Secures Software-as-a-Service Agreement with French Firm
data.description:
Vection Technologies secured a three-year software-as-a-service distribution agreement with Augmentalix with a minimum value of around AU$600,000, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

Augmentalix will distribute Vection's artificial intelligence and extended reality software portfolio in France, the filing said.

The firm's shares fell 4% on market close on Monday.

data.title: Australian shares end flat as banks offset losses in tech, commodities
data.description:
Key points:
  • Financials snap seven-session losing run
  • Technology stocks drop to near 4-month low
  • Wisetech Global logs lowest close since mid-August 2024

Australian shares ended flat on Monday as financials rose to offset losses in commodity and technology stocks, while software maker Wisetech was the biggest laggard on the benchmark index.

The S&P/ASX 200 index closed little changed at 8,308.2. The benchmark declined for the previous five sessions to end the week 3% lower.

Financials snapped a seven-session losing streak to end 1.9% higher. The "Big Four" banks gaining between 0.8% and 3%.

The Australian banks recovered after a sharp sell-off over the past eight sessions that wiped off more than A$63 billion ($40.19 billion) from their market value.

Jessica Amir, a market strategist at Moomoo Australia, said investors are buying into the dip after the top Australian banks were over-sold last week.

Technology stocks ended 6.8% lower, after dropping to the lowest since early November 2024.

Australian shares of Block slipped 12.9% and was among the top drags on the benchmark. Wisetech Global plunged 20.1%, its lowest close since mid-August 2024. It was the top laggard on S&P/ASX 200.

The sell-off in Block, after the payments solutions provider said there was competition in the market for one of their divisions, and Wisetech directors' decision to step down affected the country's technology sector, Amir said.

Adding to the gloom, miners dropped 1.6% on lower iron ore prices as more duties on Chinese steel dampened demand outlook.

BHP Group , Rio Tinto and Fortescue lost between 0.8% and 3%.

Energy stocks finished 0.8% lower, tracking a drop in oil prices.

Australian fuel retailer Ampol shed 2.6% and was among the top drags on the sub-index after reporting a steep decline in annual profit and declaring its lowest final dividend in a decade.

Gold stocks slid 2% even as bullion edged up.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index ended 1.7% lower at 12,531.72.

($1 = A$1.5676)

data.title: CBOT soybeans may test support at $10.53-1/4
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean May contract (SK25) may test support at $10.53-1/4 per bushel, a break below which could open the way toward $10.41.

The consolidation above $10.41 was shaped into a rising wedge, which consists of five small waves. It is very rare to see a wedge with seven component waves.

Most likely, the pattern has completed. Immediate resistance is at $10.60-1/2, a break above which could open the way towards $10.66-3/4 to $10.72-3/4 range.

On the daily chart, the contract seems to have completed a pullback towards a rising trendline. The pullback basically is a consolidation within the range of $10.40 to $10.60-1/4.

The support at $10.40 is expected to be retested. A break could open the way towards $10.23-3/4.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.
